raw_doc: We purchase semiconductor components primarily from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) and Samsung Electronics. The concentration of our supply chain in East Asia exposes us to geopolitical risk. Revenue in fiscal 2024 was $4.2 billion, up 18% year-over-year. Our largest customer, Apple Inc., represented 34% of net revenue. We expect revenue growth of 12-15% in fiscal 2025. Management has identified a material weakness in our internal controls over financial reporting related to the review process for complex revenue arrangements. Specifically, we did not maintain adequate documentation to support our judgment that certain multi-element arrangements were properly separated into distinct performance obligations.

icfr_material_weaknesses: material weakness in our internal controls over financial reporting related to the review process for complex revenue arrangements

guidance_tone.rubric_note: Positive forward guidance (12-15% revenue growth) with no caveats; tone is clearly optimistic despite the ICFR weakness.

rationale: Tests supplier and customer extraction alongside an ICFR material weakness; accrual/risk/critical-accounting fields are empty, exercising the empty-list path.

raw_doc: Substantial doubt exists about our ability to continue as a going concern. Our cash and cash equivalents of $8.3 million are insufficient to fund operations beyond 12 months without additional financing. We are exploring strategic alternatives including potential asset sales and equity offerings. Our principal suppliers include Foxconn Technology Group and Flex Ltd., both of which have extended payment terms to support our liquidity. We recognized an accrual of $14.7 million for estimated warranty obligations arising from a product recall announced in January 2024. Additionally, we accrued $6.2 million for restructuring charges related to the closure of our Munich facility.

accrual_flags: accrual of $14.7 million for estimated warranty obligations arising from a product recall
accrued $6.2 million for restructuring charges related to the closure of our Munich facility
going_concern: Substantial doubt exists about our ability to continue as a going concern

guidance_tone.rubric_note: Explicitly negative — management acknowledges going-concern doubt and insufficient cash runway; no optimistic guidance present.

rationale: Tests going_concern presence, two accrual flags, supplier mentions with empty customer list, and a clearly negative guidance tone.

raw_doc: We source raw materials from BASF SE and Dow Chemical Company. Key customers include Procter & Gamble, Unilever, and Johnson & Johnson. During fiscal 2023 we added a new risk factor addressing cybersecurity threats to our OT/IT network infrastructure following two ransomware incidents. We also modified our existing climate-change risk factor to incorporate SEC disclosure requirements under the new climate rules. We removed the COVID-19 supply-chain risk factor that appeared in our prior year filing as conditions have normalized. We have no going-concern uncertainties. No material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting were identified during fiscal 2023.

risk_factor_deltas: new risk factor addressing cybersecurity threats to our OT/IT network infrastructure following two ransomware incidents
modified our existing climate-change risk factor to incorporate SEC disclosure requirements under the new climate rules
removed the COVID-19 supply-chain risk factor that appeared in our prior year filing as conditions have normalized

guidance_tone.rubric_note: Neutral-to-cautious — no explicit forward guidance; tone is matter-of-fact about risk disclosures and regulatory compliance.

rationale: Tests three risk_factor_deltas of different change types plus multi-customer extraction; going_concern and ICFR fields are empty.

raw_doc: We purchase cloud infrastructure capacity from Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure. Our critical accounting estimate for goodwill impairment changed materially in fiscal 2024: we reduced the discount rate used in our DCF model from 12.5% to 10.8% following updated market data, which increased the estimated fair value of our reporting units by approximately $340 million and eliminated the prior-year headroom concern. We also changed our revenue recognition estimate for variable consideration on long-term contracts to use the expected-value method instead of the most-likely-amount method, resulting in $23 million of additional revenue recognized in Q4 2024.

critical_accounting_estimate_changes: reduced the discount rate used in our DCF model from 12.5% to 10.8% following updated market data
changed our revenue recognition estimate for variable consideration on long-term contracts to use the expected-value method instead of the most-likely-amount method

guidance_tone.rubric_note: Neutral — excerpt is disclosure-focused with no explicit management outlook or forward projections.

rationale: Tests critical_accounting_estimate_changes with two entries and infrastructure-supplier mentions with no customers or accruals.

raw_doc: We identified a significant deficiency in our revenue recognition controls for software-as-a-service arrangements. We accrued $3.1 million for potential sales-tax liabilities following a nexus study. Our top customer, Microsoft Corporation, contributed 21% of total revenue. We expect continued growth in our enterprise segment, with bookings momentum supporting mid-single-digit revenue expansion in 2025. Our principal hardware components are sourced from Jabil Inc. and Celestica Inc.

accrual_flags: accrued $3.1 million for potential sales-tax liabilities following a nexus study

guidance_tone.rubric_note: Cautiously optimistic — mid-single-digit growth expectation with bookings support; language is measured but directionally positive.

rationale: Tests a single accrual, supplier mentions, customer mention, and cautiously-optimistic guidance tone; no material weakness present.

raw_doc: Flextronics International serves as our exclusive contract manufacturer. No customers individually accounted for more than 10% of net revenue during fiscal 2023. There are no going-concern uncertainties. Management concluded that our internal controls over financial reporting are effective as of December 31, 2023. No material weaknesses or significant deficiencies were identified. Risk factors are substantially unchanged from our prior year annual report, with minor wording updates to reflect updated regulatory timelines under the EU AI Act.

guidance_tone.rubric_note: Insufficient information to characterize guidance tone — excerpt contains no forward-looking management statements.

rationale: Tests minimal-signal scenario: one supplier, no customers, no accruals, no going concern, no risk deltas, and a low-confidence guidance tone.

raw_doc: We source active pharmaceutical ingredients from Dr. Reddy's Laboratories and Lonza Group AG. Our material weakness relates to an insufficient complement of qualified accounting personnel capable of applying complex technical guidance under ASC 815 to our derivative instruments. We recorded accruals of $8.9 million for litigation contingencies following adverse arbitration rulings and $2.4 million for environmental remediation obligations at our Baytown, Texas site. Our largest customers are CVS Health and Walgreens Boots Alliance. We added a new risk factor describing the risk that compulsory licensing or drug-price negotiation under the Inflation Reduction Act could materially reduce the profitability of certain Medicare Part D products.

accrual_flags: accruals of $8.9 million for litigation contingencies following adverse arbitration rulings
$2.4 million for environmental remediation obligations at our Baytown, Texas site
risk_factor_deltas: new risk factor describing the risk that compulsory licensing or drug-price negotiation under the Inflation Reduction Act could materially reduce the profitability of certain Medicare Part D products
icfr_material_weaknesses: material weakness relates to an insufficient complement of qualified accounting personnel capable of applying complex technical guidance under ASC 815 to our derivative instruments

guidance_tone.rubric_note: No explicit forward guidance in excerpt; tone is risk-disclosure focused, consistent with a neutral-to-cautious characterization.

rationale: Pharma-sector sample with ICFR weakness, two accruals, two suppliers, two customers, and one added risk factor; high field coverage.
